commonMedications.name: Semaglutida / GLP-1 (Ozempic) [P1]
commonMedications.coverageNote: SNS covers for T2 diabetes; pensioners pay 10% (max 8 EUR/mo). ~190 EUR/mo private.

commonMedications.name: Levotiroxina (Eutirox) [P1]
commonMedications.coverageNote: SNS pensioner: 10% copay, max 8 EUR/mo cap

medicine.pharmacyAccess: Excellent. Pharmacies on nearly every block in Alicante city center with rotating 24-hour duty pharmacies (farmacias de guardia). Many medications available without prescription that would require one elsewhere.
medicine.prescriptionSystem: SNS electronic prescription (receta electrónica) linked to SIP health card. Doctor issues renewable prescriptions for chronic conditions (up to 1 year). Pensioners with income under €18,000 pay 0% copay; €18,000-100,000 pay 10% capped at €8.23/month.
medicine.insuranceCoverage: Public SNS covers most prescription drugs after gaining residency. Private insurance (required for NLV visa) typically covers 80-100% of medications. During initial NLV period before public coverage, private plan is primary.

sources.title: Spanish Ministry of Health – Pharmaceutical Copayment

annualExamCost.notes: SNS covers basic eye exams. Private ophthalmologists charge €40-80. Optical shops offer free basic checks.

vision.insuranceCoverage: SNS covers ophthalmology referrals and cataract surgery. Glasses and contact lenses are NOT covered by public healthcare. Private insurance often includes one pair of glasses every 2 years or a €100-150 annual allowance.
vision.specialistWaitTime: Public ophthalmology wait: 2-6 months for non-urgent. Private: 1-2 weeks. Cataract surgery public wait can be 3-9 months.
vision.seniorConsiderations: High prevalence of age-related macular degeneration and glaucoma monitoring. Annual retinal exams recommended. SNS covers diabetic retinopathy screening. Alicante has Hospital General Universitario with full ophthalmology department.

annualCleaningCost.notes: Dental is NOT covered by SNS public healthcare (except extractions and emergencies for children). Private dental insurance or pay-as-you-go.

commonProcedures.coverageNote: Not covered by SNS. Private dental plans cover 1-2 per year.
Not covered by SNS. Private plans cover 50-70% typically.
Not covered by SNS. Private plans partially cover.
Basic extractions covered by SNS in limited cases. Private plans cover fully.
Not covered by SNS or most private plans. Spain is popular for dental tourism due to competitive implant pricing.
Not covered by SNS. Some private plans offer partial coverage.

dental.insuranceCoverage: SNS does NOT cover adult dental care (only emergency extractions). Private dental insurance runs €15-40/month and typically includes 1-2 cleanings, X-rays, and discounts on procedures. Dental clinics like Vitaldent and Sanitas Dental offer membership plans.
dental.dentistAvailability: Excellent in Alicante. Many private dental clinics, some with English-speaking staff catering to expats. No referral needed for private dentists. Dental tourism from Northern Europe keeps quality high and prices competitive.

sources.title: Consejo General de Dentistas de España

categories.name: Dining Out
categories.seniorDiscounts: Menu del día excellent value (€10-14 for 3 courses). Small tip 5-10% customary.
categories.notes: Lunch for 2: menú del día ~€24 + 5% tip = ~€25, 2x/week = ~€217/mo = ~$245/mo

categories.name: NFL RedZone
categories.notes: DAZN NFL Game Pass €19.99/mo during season (Sep-Feb). Annualized ~€10/mo (~$11). Includes RedZone, live games, NFL Network.

monthlyPass.notes: TAM (Transporte Alicante Metropolitano) monthly Bono. TRAM Alicante (tram/light rail) has separate tariff. Combined bus+tram available.

publicTransit.coverage: TAM urban bus network covers Alicante city well. TRAM Alicante connects to Benidorm, Denia along the coast. Cercanías rail to Elche and Murcia.
publicTransit.seniorDiscount: Pensionistas with Tarjeta TAM get up to 50% discount. TRAM offers reduced Bono for pensionistas and 65+. Must register with valid residency.

rideShare.availability: Uber and Cabify operate in Alicante. Regular taxis also plentiful with taxi stands throughout the city.
rideShare.notes: Taxi from airport to city center approximately €18-22. Uber/Cabify slightly cheaper.

registration.notes: Impuesto de circulación (road tax) varies by engine size. Initial matriculación tax 4.75% for used imports.

insurance.notes: Third-party (terceros) from €25/mo. Comprehensive (todo riesgo) €50-80/mo. Higher for non-Spanish license holders initially.

fuel.notes: Gasoline €1.50-1.65/L. Diesel €1.40-1.55/L. Moderate driving needs in coastal area.

parking.notes: Resident parking (SER zona azul) much cheaper with empadronamiento. Underground garages €60-120/mo.

tolls.notes: AP-7 motorway now free. Some remaining toll sections on other autopistas. Minimal for local driving.

carOwnership.seniorDiscounts: No specific senior discounts on car ownership. Tarjeta Dorada for Renfe trains (25-40% off) is the main transport discount.

transportation.walkability: Excellent in Alicante city center and Explanada de España area. Flat along the coast, hilly near castle. Most daily errands walkable in central neighborhoods. Beach promenade extends several kilometers.

cycling.infrastructure: Growing network of bike lanes along the coast and through the city. Alicante BiciAli public bike share system. Flat terrain along the coast is ideal for cycling.
cycling.notes: Weather is cycling-friendly year-round. Hills near the castle area can be challenging.

electricVehicle.purchaseNotes: Dacia Spring ~€22K, Renault Megane E-Tech ~€35K, Tesla Model 3 ~€40K, Hyundai Ioniq 5 ~€45K. MOVES III incentives available.
electricVehicle.incentiveNotes: Plan MOVES III: up to €7,000 subsidy for new EV purchase (scrapping old vehicle required for max). Reduced registration tax (0% for EVs vs 4.75% for ICE). Some municipalities reduce IVTM (road tax) up to 75%.

chargingHome.notes: Electricity ~€0.15-0.25/kWh (variable by hour, use supervalle/nocturna tariff). Solar potential excellent on Costa Blanca.

chargingPublic.notes: Iberdrola, Endesa, Repsol networks. Some free chargers at shopping centers. Ionity on highways.

insurance.notes: Competitive rates. Línea Directa, Mapfre, Mutua Madrileña. EVs sometimes cheaper to insure due to lower theft rates.

maintenance.notes: ITV (MOT) every 2 yrs after 4 yrs. No oil changes. Lower labor rates. Spanish workshops increasingly EV-capable.

registration.notes: IVTM (impuesto vehículos) reduced 50-75% for EVs in many municipalities. Alicante offers 75% reduction.

electricVehicle.depreciationNotes: EV market growing fast in Spain. Government incentives support resale values. Solar charging potential reduces long-term costs.
electricVehicle.totalMonthlyNotes: Includes: charging (€35), insurance (€50), maintenance (€33/mo avg), registration (€1/mo). Excludes: purchase/loan payment, depreciation.

discounts.details: Must have residency (empadronamiento) and pensioner status or be 65+. Apply at TAM/TRAM offices with SIP card.
Available to anyone 60+. Costs €6/year. Valid on AVE, Cercanías, Media Distancia, and Larga Distancia.
Automatic once registered in SNS as pensioner. Applied at pharmacy with electronic prescription.

discounts.description: Reduced entry at museums and cultural venues
discounts.details: MARQ €2 reduced rate (normally €5). Many museums free for 65+. First Sunday of month often free for all.
Municipal sports centers offer Tarifa Jubilado. Must be empadronado.

discounts.discount: Subsidized courses at University of Alicante
discounts.details: Cultural, language, history, and technology courses for 55+. Very affordable annual enrollment.

discounts.discount: Higher personal allowance for 65+ (€1,150 extra) and 75+ (€1,400 extra)
discounts.details: Applied automatically in IRPF tax return. Reduces taxable income.

plans.name: Google Fi Flexible (US Number)
plans.notes: Line 1 $20 + Line 2 $15 = $35 base. Data $10/GB × 3GB = $30. Works in Spain at same rate. Keeps US number for banking/2FA.

plans.name: Digi Mobil (Local)
plans.notes: Digi Mobil €7/mo per line (21% IVA included). 5GB data, unlimited calls in Spain. Cheapest quality option. ~$17/mo for 2 lines.

housing.taxNotes: No VAT (IVA) on residential rent in Spain. IBI (property tax) is landlord responsibility. Basura (garbage collection) ~€90-110/year often charged to tenant. Community fees (comunidad) for urbanizaciones passed to tenant. Tenant must register on the padrón (municipal register).
housing.leaseTerms: 5-year lease minimum (Ley de Arrendamientos Urbanos, LAU 2023 reform). Landlord cannot terminate before 5 years unless personal need. Security deposit: 1 month legally (fianza). Rent increases capped by INE index (max 3% in 2024, 2% in 2025).
housing.marketNotes: Costa Blanca has excellent availability of 2BR villas/chalets, especially in urbanizaciones (gated communities) outside city center. Playa de San Juan, Campello, Mutxamel offer good value. Many come with pool access.

stores.name: Consum
stores.notes: Regional Valencia chain, strong in Alicante

items.name: Chicken (whole/thighs)
items.notes: Primary protein for both adults & dog

items.name: Ground Turkey / Beef
items.notes: Lean ground for dog food base

items.name: Organ Meat (liver/heart)
items.notes: Essential for balanced home-cooked dog diet

healthcare.coverageSummary: Private insurance required for NLV visa (~2 years). After permanent residency, can access SNS public healthcare. Pre-existing condition loading 20-40% on private premiums.

costs.notes: ~€120-140/mo. Pre-existing loading for AMD + obesity. Age surcharge for 60+.
Private: €300-500/injection, 6-8/yr. Insurance covers partially. Switch to SNS ASAP.
SNS covers 100% once you have residency. No copay for hospital procedures.
~€100-120/mo. Pre-existing loading for diabetes. Age surcharge.
Glucose strips partially covered by private insurance.

shared.notes: Private insurance includes basic dental. Complex work has copays.
SNS: pensioners pay max 10% copay, capped at €8/mo for pharmacy.

healthcare.notes: Costs shown for initial private insurance period. After ~2 years residency, switch to SNS reduces costs significantly. SNS pensioner copays are minimal (10% up to €8/mo).

blackHairCare.label: Black Hair Care
blackHairCare.notes: Alicante has a growing African community. Salons are found in the Carolinas and Benalua neighborhoods. Fewer options than larger Spanish cities.

providers.name: Afro Beauty Peluqueria
providers.notes: Braids, locs, weaves, natural hair care. Experienced with all textures.

providers.name: Belleza Africana
providers.notes: African and Caribbean hair care. Relaxers, color, protective styles.

whiteHairCare.label: White/Caucasian Hair Care
whiteHairCare.notes: Spanish peluquerias are generally affordable. Many good options along Avenida Maisonnave and in the Ensanche area.

providers.name: Llongueras
providers.notes: Premium Spanish salon chain. Expert colorists. Highlights, balayage. Known for quality.

providers.name: Marco Aldany
providers.notes: Good value chain. Cuts and color at moderate prices. Walk-in friendly.

mensBigTall.label: Men's Big & Tall Clothing
mensBigTall.notes: Spain uses EU sizing. Kiabi and Primark carry extended sizes. El Corte Inglés has the best in-store selection for larger sizes.

providers.name: El Corte Inglés
providers.notes: Spain's premier department store. Extended sizes section. Tailoring available. Best in-store selection.

providers.name: Kiabi / Primark
providers.notes: Budget extended sizes. Primark up to 3XL. Kiabi to 5XL. Very affordable.

womensClothing.label: Women's Regular Clothing
womensClothing.notes: Alicante is very affordable for clothing. Zara, Mango, and H&M on Avenida Maisonnave. El Corte Inglés for more upscale options.

providers.name: El Corte Inglés
providers.notes: Full women's department. Spanish sizes 34-52. Alterations available.

providers.name: Zara / Mango (Maisonnave)
providers.notes: Zara and Mango are headquartered in Spain — best selection and prices here.

shoes.label: Shoes
shoes.notes: Spain is a major shoe-producing country. Excellent quality at lower prices than US. Elche (30 min away) is the shoe capital.

providers.name: El Corte Inglés (shoe dept)
providers.notes: Excellent shoe department. Spanish brands (Pikolinos, Camper). Extended sizes.

providers.name: Pikolinos Factory Outlet (Elche)
providers.notes: Famous Spanish comfort shoe brand. Factory prices. Wide widths available. Worth the trip.
